title: Centaurea balbisiana
taxonomy: Centaurea balbisiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Centaurea, which comprises over 600 species of thistles and knapweeds. The species is further categorized as part of the subgenus Centaurea section Cynarastrum. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. The classification of Centaurea balbisiana reflects its unique characteristics and its relationship to other plants within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Centaurea balbisiana is known for its potential medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that extracts from Centaurea balbisiana may possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Further studies are required to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and to develop safe and effective treatments. The medicinal uses of Centaurea balbisiana are primarily based on its traditional use in folk medicine.

The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. parts: Centaurea balbisiana. The medicinal parts of Centaurea balbisiana are the flowers, leaves, and roots, which have been traditionally used in various folk remedies.
